title: abutilon piurense
intro: Abutilon piurense, also known as the flowering mapou tree or abutilon, is a flowering plant species native to the Pacific Islands and New Caledonia. It is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that aid in wound healing and treating various infections. The health benefits of Abutilon piurense include reducing fever, treating respiratory issues, and providing relief from arthritis and rheumatism. Herbal preparations of the plant are typically made by infusing the leaves in hot water or using the bark in traditional medicine, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
